summary: An assembly test failed: tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs produced unexpected assembly output, causing the assembly-llvm compiletest suite to fail for target wasm32-wasip1.
root_cause: The assembly test tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs failed due to a mismatch between expected and generated assembly (assembly check failed), causing the compiletest assembly-llvm suite to report a failure for target wasm32-wasip1.

evidence.reason: Shows an assembly test check marker (indicating a diff/mismatch region in the assembly test).
Identifies the exact assembly test that ended with a failure.

evidence.quote: test result: FAILED. 550 passed; 1 failed; 103 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 15.38s
evidence.reason: Confirms that one test failed in the test run.

evidence.quote: Some tests failed in compiletest suite=assembly-llvm mode=assembly host=x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu target=wasm32-wasip1
evidence.reason: Shows the failing test was part of the assembly-llvm compiletest suite targeting wasm32-wasip1.

evidence.quote: Bootstrap failed while executing `--stage 2 test --host= --target wasm32-wasip1 tests/run-make tests/run-make-cargo tests/ui tests/mir-opt tests/codegen-units tests/codegen-llvm tests/assembly-llvm library/core`
evidence.reason: The bootstrap/test driver aborted because the test suite (including assembly-llvm) failed.

suggested_fix: Re-run the assembly test locally to reproduce and inspect the assembly diff (e.g. run the failing bootstrap command shown in the log). Then either update the expected assembly in tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs to match the current generated output or adjust codegen/target feature changes that caused the assembly to change. The bootstrap command from the log: `--stage 2 test --host= --target wasm32-wasip1 ... tests/assembly-llvm`.
